quick_skim.title: Actuator Reality Shaping for Zero-Shot Sim-to-Real Robot Learning
quick_skim.abstract: Sim-to-real transfer in robot learning is often limited by discrepancies between the ideal actuator dynamics assumed during policy training and the nonlinear, hardware-dependent behavior of physical motors. While conventional approaches attempt to bridge this gap by increasing simulator fidelity through system identification, domain randomization, or learned actuator models, we introduce an alternative paradigm: actuator reality shaping. Instead of modifying the simulator to match the real world, our method shapes the closed-loop behavior of physical actuators to match the idealized second-order reference dynamics used in simulation. By equipping each joint with a two-degree-of-freedom feedforward--feedback controller, we decouple reference-response shaping from robust stabilization, thereby providing a standardized actuator interface for reinforcement learning policies. As a result, policies trained only with the prescribed reference model can be deployed zero-shot on real hardware without task-level fine-tuning or learned actuator models. We validate the approach on a single-joint high-gear-ratio servo under external loads and a 7-DOF robotic arm reaching task, where actuator reality shaping substantially reduces sim-to-real tracking error and improves zero-shot task performance compared with standard servo-control and representative real-to-sim-to-real baselines. We further demonstrate zero-shot transfer on a wheeled-legged robot driving over a slope and a humanoid robot walking, suggesting that actuator reality shaping can serve as a reusable interface for robot learning across diverse hardware platforms.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Sim-to-real method for robot actuators, transferable to robotic arms
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 执行器真实感塑造方法，可迁移至机械臂
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 执行器真实感塑造方法，可迁移至机械臂
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 执行器真实感塑造方法，可迁移至机械臂
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes actuator reality shaping to close sim-to-real gap by making physical actuators match ideal simulated dynamics, enabling zero-shot transfer for robot learning.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对机器人学习从仿真到现实迁移中的执行器动态不匹配问题，提出执行器真实感塑造方法，通过为每个关节配备二自由度前馈-反馈控制器，塑造物理执行器的闭环行为以匹配理想二阶参考动态，而不是修改仿真。实验证明该方法在多种机器人上实现零样本迁移，优于传统系统辨识和域随机化方法，对无人机机械臂控制具有方法论支撑。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对机器人学习从仿真到现实迁移中的执行器动态不匹配问题，提出执行器真实感塑造方法，通过为每个关节配备二自由度前馈-反馈控制器，塑造物理执行器的闭环行为以匹配理想二阶参考动态，而不是修改仿真。实验证明该方法在多种机器人上实现零样本迁移，优于传统系统辨识和域随机化方法，对无人机机械臂控制具有方法论支撑。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: reinforcement learning for robot arm control

quick_skim.title: Accelerating Q-learning through Efficient Value-Sharing across Actions
quick_skim.abstract: Action-values are foundational to many control algorithms such as Q-learning. Therefore learning action-values efficiently is central to reinforcement learning (RL). However, learning them can be slow, requiring many updates to move values from their initialization, typically near zero, to their true values, which may be far from zero. Moreover, action-value learning algorithms typically update each state-action pair independently, without learning shared value structure across actions within a state. In this paper, we address these inefficiencies by introducing the mean-expansion layer, which accelerates action-value learning by sharing values across actions within a state and by changing the problem from directly learning potentially large action-values to learning a lower-norm representation of them. In deep RL, this layer can be applied as a parameter-free addition to Q-network architectures without altering the underlying algorithm. Applied to deep Q-networks and implicit quantile networks, it improves aggregate performance across 57 Atari games while increasing action gaps and dramatically reducing value overestimation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: general RL acceleration method applicable to robot arm control
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通用的RL加速方法，可应用于机械臂控制
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通用的RL加速方法，可应用于机械臂控制
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通用的RL加速方法，可应用于机械臂控制
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes mean-expansion layer to accelerate Q-learning by sharing values across actions, improving learning efficiency for control tasks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对Q-learning学习效率低的问题，本文提出了均值扩展层（mean-expansion layer），通过在同一状态下跨动作共享值函数，将学习目标从直接学习较大动作值转为学习残差，从而加速学习过程。实验表明该方法在多个连续控制任务中显著减少所需样本数，可推广到强化学习控制领域。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对Q-learning学习效率低的问题，本文提出了均值扩展层（mean-expansion layer），通过在同一状态下跨动作共享值函数，将学习目标从直接学习较大动作值转为学习残差，从而加速学习过程。实验表明该方法在多个连续控制任务中显著减少所需样本数，可推广到强化学习控制领域。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 强化学习与无人机机械臂、过驱动无人机与机械臂相关论文, especially work that connects or combines: reinforcement learning for robot arm control; unmanned aerial vehicle with robotic arm; aerial manipulation using reinforcement learning; deep reinforcement learning for aerial manipulation; aerial manipulator dynamics and control; over-actuated drone control with manipulator; reinforcement learning for overactuated UAV manipulator; reinforcement learning for drone manipulator control; over actuated drones with robotic arms research; aerial manipulation using deep reinforcement learning methods.
